title: chelidonium majus subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Chelidonium majus subsp. grandiflorum, also known as greater celandine, is a perennial plant belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of greater celandine include its ability to support liver and digestive health, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Greater celandine can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including skin issues and gastrointestinal problems.
